location.office_time_required: minimum of 2 days in office per week, with most teams in office between 2-3 days a week

level_signals: 4+ years working closely with product teams
'Staff' in title signals senior individual contributor above Senior
Mentorship and leadership of complex projects explicitly required
Comp range CAD $120K-$160K consistent with Staff IC level
Cross-team impact across all of HelloTech, not limited to one squad

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Extensive experience in quantitative analysis roles, ideally including having worked closely with product teams for at least 4 years.
Excellent technical skills applying expert level SQL and Python for both analysis and efficient manipulation of large data sets.
Expert level knowledge of data visualisation tools like Tableau which we use to create compelling visualisations to convey complex data.
Extensive experience working with product stakeholders in setting up A|B experiments, interpreting results and deep diving to understand the impact of particular experiments.
Excellent communication skills, taking complex business problems, articulating these as analytical problems and presenting your findings in a clear and precise way.
Extensive leadership of complex projects you have led, capability building within your team, and through mentorship or direct people management.
You are outcome oriented, and can take a business challenge, create actionable analytical insights against it, and align stakeholders on actions to create business value.
Knowledge of Curser and AI Analytics

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Leverage Cursor AI to automate and expand analytical capabilities and insight generation for the product organisation.
A change mindset, the status quo is not an impediment

scope.surface: Product Analytics across HelloTech (Growth domain); cross-squad impact on all product and engineering teams
scope.business_model: B2C subscription meal kit (HelloFresh, Chefs Plate, Factor)
primary_metrics_inferred: Digital product growth and user experience improvement
Evidence-based decision making adoption across product organisation
core_responsibilities: Leverage Cursor AI to automate and expand analytical capabilities and insight generation for the product organisation
Collaborate from a technical perspective with data product managers and data engineers to create reusable data products
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather requirements and define project objectives while utilising project management skills to plan and execute data-driven projects
Partner widely across the Senior Product organisation to set strategic priorities and promote a culture rooted in evidence-based decision making
Communicate actionable insights clearly across the organisation highlighting the 'so-whats' of your analyses in order to drive the growth of our business
Explore and analyse customer behaviour and product performance data in order to detect growth opportunities for the HelloFresh digital product by improving user experience
Guide and mentor other Product Analysts within our team, defining best practices and providing support to help individuals and the team grow in effectiveness
Review as a technical expert the implementations of multiple teams across domains, continuously improving the deliverables of your teams

green_flags: Hybrid policy is clearly articulated (minimum 2 days in office per week)
Role has broad, cross-team impact rather than being siloed to one squad
Explicit mentorship and leadership scope signals genuine career growth opportunity
Active vacancy with explicit statement this is an existing open role
red_flags: Currency is CAD, not USD — candidates expecting USD comp should adjust expectations
Visa sponsorship and work authorisation not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Cursor AI listed as a 'must have' knowledge area but is a relatively new tool — may reflect inflated expectations
Typo in JD ('Curser' vs 'Cursor') suggests possible lack of careful editing
Cursor AI requirement appears in both must_haves (as 'Knowledge of') and responsibilities — slight redundancy; hard vs soft boundary is blurry
AI-integrated screening tools disclosed — candidates should be aware of automated filtering

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Staff' title, comp range, and cross-team ownership language)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and benefits mentioning HelloFresh, Chefs Plate, Factor)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
compensation.base_range_cad (added as a non-schema extension to capture CAD currency accurately; base_range_usd left null as only CAD stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred false; role is Toronto-based hybrid)
nice_to_haves[0] (Cursor AI automation responsibility treated as soft/nice-to-have separately from hard 'knowledge' requirement, as it describes a day-to-day activity rather than a prerequisite)
ambiguities: Cursor AI appears both as a stated 'must have' knowledge requirement ('Knowledge of Curser and AI Analytics') and as a primary job responsibility ('Leverage Cursor AI to automate...'). Treated the knowledge requirement as hard and the usage activity as a soft/nice-to-have to avoid full duplication.
JD title says 'Growth' but the role is a Product Analyst, not a traditional Growth PM or Growth Marketer. Archetype set to 'Growth (non-PM)' as closest match.

The JD says 'ideally including having worked closely with product teams for at least 4 years' — the word 'ideally' introduces softness, but the surrounding 'Extensive experience' framing and 4-year minimum suggest hard intent. Treated as hard and flagged here.
Visa sponsorship and work authorisation status not mentioned anywhere in the JD.
